description: On-demand local inference runtime manifest. These binaries are intentionally NOT committed to git and NOT shipped in the pip wheel (see setup.py EXCLUDE_PATTERNS and MANIFEST.in prune). On first use (or via `khy runtime install`) they are downloaded per-platform, verified by SHA256, and placed at their original paths under services/backend/bin/. URLs default to official upstream releases; set the KHY_RUNTIME_MIRROR_BASE env var to fetch the same filenames from a mirror (useful for restricted networks / air-gapped hosts). The sha256 (and, if needed, sourceSubdir) values are finalized by scripts/release/pin-runtime-binaries.js. A platform entry of null, or a missing/empty sha256, means there is no pinned binary for that platform: the runtime silently falls back to the system-installed binary.

ollama-runner.description: Ollama inference runner: the `ollama` multi-call binary plus lib/ollama/*.so. Invoked by the CLI as `ollama runner --ollama-engine ...`.
ollama-runner.sentinelNote: Runtime-level default sentinel (POSIX `bin/ollama`). A platform entry may override it with its own `sentinel` when the upstream archive lays the binary out differently (the Windows zip ships `ollama.exe` at the archive root, not under bin/). The provisioner resolves `plat.sentinel || runtime.sentinel` BEFORE the present/absent fast-path so each platform is detected correctly.
ollama-runner.versionNote: The vendored runner reports version 0.0.0, so the exact upstream version is not derivable from the binary. IMPORTANT: running qwen3.5 / qwen3-next requires a runner whose Go engine registers the qwen35 architecture — the vendored binary already does (its strings contain `qwen3nextqwen35moe`). When arming sha256 via pin-runtime-binaries.js, bump this tag (and the per-platform URLs) to an upstream release new enough to support qwen3-next; the v0.6.8 URLs here are layout placeholders, not a qwen35-capable tag. The pin script fails loudly on a wrong/nonexistent tag so it can be corrected.

darwin-arm64.sentinelNote: Inherits the runtime-level `bin/ollama`. If pin --verify-layout reports the darwin universal archive lays the binary at the root (`ollama`) instead, set `sentinel`: "ollama" here.

darwin-x64.sentinelNote: Same universal ollama-darwin.tgz as darwin-arm64. Inherits the runtime-level `bin/ollama`; override to "ollama" if pin --verify-layout finds a root-level binary.

win32-x64.sentinelNote: The Windows zip ships ollama.exe at the archive root (no bin/ prefix), so this overrides the runtime-level `bin/ollama`.

llama-cpp.description: llama.cpp CLI suite (build b9049): llama-cli, llama-quantize, llama-server, llama-completion, etc. plus shared libraries.

linux-x64.sourceSubdirNote: Hint only; the provisioner auto-detects the real payload root by locating the sentinel if this is wrong.
